latest_messages.headline: Midday: steady, no changes — now up on the day while the market is down
latest_messages.body: Nothing to trade — no buys, no sells, no changes to our safety nets. I pulled our live account, checked all three stocks and their trailing stops, and everything is healthy and resting. The tables turned in our favor since the open: the S&P 500 is still off ~0.55%, but our account ticked UP +0.10% — so we're beating the market by about two-thirds of a percent today. That gap in our favor is 'alpha' (extra return over just owning the index), the number that matters most long-term. Same reason as this morning: none of our three stocks are tech (Lilly drugs, JPMorgan bank, Deere farm equipment), and JPMorgan bounced back from its rough open as banks recovered. Nothing new clears our strict buy bar (two solid reasons + a 70+ score), so I'm not forcing anything. Later today is my weekly review: full week scorecard after the close, plus a clear cash-pile question for you. Nothing needs your input now. Equity $101,484 (+$104/+0.10% today, +1.48% since our $100K start); LLY +9.3%, JPM +4.2%, DE +1.3% (combined open gains ~+$2,042); cash ~$60,168 (~59%), 0 of 3 weekly buys used.

latest_messages.headline: Open: no trades — tech-led selloff, but our non-tech, cash-heavy book is beating the market
latest_messages.body: No trades at the open — no buys, no sells, no changes. The market opened down hard, led by tech and chip stocks (S&P 500 off ~1.2% early), but we're down only ~0.37% — none of our three holdings are tech (Eli Lilly drugs, JPMorgan bank, Deere farm equipment) and we're ~60% cash, which cushions a red day. Falling far less than the market is exactly the point of being selective. JPM is today's soft spot (−2% with the banks) but still in profit and nowhere near its safety net. Nothing new clears our strict buy bar (two solid reasons + score 70+, the 16th straight quiet scan), so I didn't force anything. Later today is my weekly review: full week scorecard after the close, plus a clear question for you about our big cash pile — keep waiting or start deploying. Nothing needs your input right now. Equity ~$101,002 (+1.0% since our $100K start), cash ~$60,168 (~60%), 0 of 3 weekly buys used, all three stops resting.

latest_messages.headline: Pre-market plan: no buys — tech-led selloff overseas, but our non-tech book is well placed
latest_messages.body: Research-only routine (market still closed), so no trades. I ran my four research assistants in parallel. Overseas markets fell hard overnight, but it's concentrated in technology and chip stocks (Japan -4%, Korea -6%) — the rest of the market is barely down. Two things work in our favor: all three of our stocks (Eli Lilly, JPMorgan, Deere) are OUTSIDE tech, so they're not in the line of fire, and we're ~59% cash, which cushions a red day. All three holdings healthy and on trailing safety nets (auto-sells that follow the price up): LLY +8.8%, JPM +4.1%, DE +1.6% — none near triggering. On buying: nothing clears our strict two-reasons-plus-70-score bar (15th quiet scan). American Express is still closest (one confirmed reason; waits for its Jul 24 report). Johnson & Johnson posted a genuine beat-and-raise this week — I've added it to the watch bench, but one reason isn't enough to buy. Today is my weekly review: I'll send the full week scorecard after the close and raise a cash-deployment question for you. ~59% cash, 0 of 3 weekly buys used. +1.4% since our $100K start.

latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $101,399 · day -0.43% · SPY -0.52% · alpha +0.09% — a red day, but we beat the market
latest_messages.body: It was a down day for stocks across the board and we dipped too, finishing at $101,399, off about $436 (-0.43%). The good news: the S&P 500 fell more, -0.52%, so even on a losing day we lost LESS than the market — that gap in our favor is our alpha (extra return vs. just owning the index), +0.09% today. Small, but it's the number that matters long-term, and it's green. What moved us: GE was sold this morning for a +$671 profit — its trailing stop (an auto-sell that rises with the price to lock in gains) did its job before the earnings dip could erase the win, and that cash is banked. Eli Lilly (+8.5%) and Deere (+1.5% on the day) held us up; JPMorgan was the soft spot, down ~1%, but still +4% for us and nowhere near its safety net. We're sitting on ~59% cash — more than I like idle. I'm hunting for a strong place to put it but won't force a weak buy just to be invested. Tomorrow is my weekly review: full week scorecard, and I'll decide whether to bring you a clear cash-deployment question. Nothing needs your input tonight; all three holdings have resting safety nets. Equity +1.40% since our $100K start; open gains total +$1,947 across LLY, JPM, DE.
